task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Role-Based Access Control** (Level 8)

Build an API with role-based access. Users have roles (admin/editor/viewer). POST /api/articles creates articles (editors and admins only). DELETE /api/articles/:id deletes (admins only). GET /api/articles lists all (anyone with auth). Viewers can read but not create or delete.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for javascript backend

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique
  password, required
  role, required

create a
final_source:
/*
ROLE-BASED ACCESS CONTROL API (Level 8)
Users have roles (admin, editor, viewer). POST /articles (editors+admins only),
DELETE /articles/:id (admins only), GET /articles (authenticated).
*/

build for javascript backend
database is local memory

owner is 'admin@test.com'
allow signup and login

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique
  password, required
  role, required

create a Articles table:
  title, required
  body
  author_id

# POST /api/register — register user with custom role
when user calls POST /api/register sending data:
  validate data:
    email is text, required, matches email
    password is text, required
    role is text, required
  saved = save data to Users
  send back saved with status 201

# POST /api/login — login user
when user calls POST /api/login sending data:
  validate data:
    email is text, required, matches email
    password is text, required
  user = look up User with this email
  if user:
    send back {token: 'jwt_token_here'} with status 200
  send back {error: 'Invalid credentials'} with status 401

# POST /api/articles — create article (editors and admins only)
when user calls POST /api/articles sending data:
  requires login
  validate data:
    title must not be empty
  saved = save data to Articles
  send back saved with status 201

# DELETE /api/articles/:id — delete article (admins only)
when user calls DELETE /api/articles/:id:
  requires login
  requires role 'admin'
  delete from Articles where id is this id
  send back 'Article deleted' with status 200

# GET /api/articles — list all articles (authenticated users)
when user calls GET /api/articles:
  requires login
  articles = get all Articles
  send back articles

test 'POST /api/register with admin role':
  call POST /api/register sending {email: 'admin@test.com', password: 'pass1234', role: 'admin'}
  expect response status 201

test 'POST /api/login returns token':
  call POST /api/login sending {email: 'admin@test.com', password: 'pass1234'}
  expect response status 200
  expect response has 'token'

test 'GET /api/articles without auth returns 401':
  call GET /api/articles
  expect response status 401
